  "findings": [
    "Slings are rigged over unprotected sharp edges, creating an immediate risk of sling failure and dropped load."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift immediately.",
    "Lower the load if suspended, or secure it.",
    "Install engineered softeners, padding, or corner protectors to protect the slings from the sharp edges before proceeding."
  ],
  "rationale": "Rigging slings must be protected from sharp edges, corners, and protrusions to prevent cutting, abrasion, and catastrophic failure. Operating with unprotected edges violates basic safety standards and manufacturer specifications.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)(9)"
  ]
